Culture.detailedSummary: Text settings Story text Size Small Standard Large Width * Standard Wide Links Standard Orange * Subscribers only Learn more Minimize to nav The crew of Artemis II spoke with the media on Thursday, six days after returning to Earth following their mission around the Moon. After a news conference, the astronauts gave a handful of interviews, and Ars was able to speak with Orion’s pilot, Victor Glover. Glover now stands at the vanguard of our modern Apollo program, named Artemis, which aims to return humans to the Moon and establish a semi-permanent base there. Glover, an accomplished naval aviator, first went to space in November 2020 as the pilot on the first operational Crew Dragon mission to the International Space Station. Two years after he landed back on Earth, Glover was assigned to the Artemis II mission and tasked with a majority of the test piloting of the Orion spacecraft during the outbound and return journey from the Moon.

In a non-Newtonian fluid, the viscosity changes in response to an app...
Culture.detailedSummary: Text settings Story text Size Small Standard Large Width * Standard Wide Links Standard Orange * Subscribers only Learn more Minimize to nav Oobleck has long been my favorite example of a non-Newtonian fluid, and I’m not alone. In a non-Newtonian fluid, the viscosity changes in response to an applied strain or shearing force, thereby straddling the boundary between liquid and solid behavior. But for non-Newtonian fluids like oobleck, the viscosity changes when a shearing force is applied. By making one bulk measurement, they could track the emergence of the interactions between nanoparticles (the “microscopic frictional contacts”) and correlate that with when the shear thickening behavior kicked in and the liquid switched from liquid to solid behavior. Kim et al., 2023 The team found that the friction between particles was critical to the liquid-to-solid transition, identifying a tipping point when the friction reached a certain level and the viscosity abruptly increased.

China has conducted what appears to be a wet dress rehearsal, or fueling test, for its Long March 10B rocket, paving ...
News.detailedSummary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched a Cygnus cargo ship from Northrop Grumman on Saturday, kicking off a resupply flight to the International Space Station, Spaceflight Now reports. China has conducted what appears to be a wet dress rehearsal, or fueling test, for its Long March 10B rocket, paving the way for a potential launch within weeks, Space News reports. The US Space Force is still dealing with the near-term implications of the second grounding of United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rocket in less than two years, Ars reports. The Vulcan rocket is one of the two primary launch vehicles the Space Force uses to put satellites into orbit, alongside SpaceX’s Falcon 9. Eric Zarybnisky, head of Space Systems Command’s Space Access office, said the military is “working through a significant number” of potential additional rocket swaps from Vulcan to another launch vehicle, likely SpaceX’s Falcon 9 or Falcon Heavy.

Transformer models Transformer models are a type of neural network specialized in natural language processing: identifyi...
Technology.detailedSummary: Authors Ivan Belcic Staff writer Cole Stryker Staff Editor, AI Models IBM Think What is GPT (generative pretrained transformer)? Generative pretrained transformers (GPTs) are a family of large language models (LLMs) based on a transformer deep learning architecture. Transformer models including GPT and BERT have powered many notable developments in generative AI since then, with OpenAI’s ChatGPT chatbot taking center stage. The power of GPT models comes from two key aspects: Generative pretraining that teaches the model to detect patterns in unlabeled data, then apply those patterns to new inputs. Transformer models Transformer models are a type of neural network specialized in natural language processing: identifying the intent and meaning in a text-based input.

Technology.shortSummary: And with that introduction, I want to describe the next part of our open source evolution. Forem is open source software for building communities.
Technology.detailedSummary: We don’t want to just power community software, we want to empower community. And with that introduction, I want to describe the next part of our open source evolution. Forem is open source software for building communities. As an organization and open source community that works in a distributed, asynchronous style, it's only natural to be somewhat sprawling. Some of the first Forems we launch will be meta-forems designed to help facilitate the software development process as well as the administration, moderation and promotion of individual communities.

Environment.shortSummary: Proctor In a significant milestone, the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) has completed its 3D map of the Universe—the highest resolution of any such map yet achieved—on schedule and with more data than expected, the collaboration announced today. Analyses of DESI data from earlier runs...
Environment.detailedSummary: Proctor In a significant milestone, the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) has completed its 3D map of the Universe—the highest resolution of any such map yet achieved—on schedule and with more data than expected, the collaboration announced today. Analyses of DESI data from earlier runs have already produced exciting hints of new physics—namely that the Universe’s dark energy, rather than being constant, might vary over time. So the Universe should be accelerating much faster than it is if the dark energy is, essentially, the cosmological constant. That data is then sliced into chunks to determine how fast the Universe was expanding at each point of time in the past to better model how dark energy was affecting that expansion. But finishing the original planned survey, on time, with significantly more data than expected, is a major achievement in itself.

Health.detailedSummary: The retraction came from Neurobiology of Aging, which removed a 2011 paper claiming to show that a version of a protein called amyloid-β was responsible for memory loss in Alzheimer’s disease. Alzheimer noted that her brain tissue contained plaques, which had previously been seen in other dementia patients, as well as tangles of nerve fibers, which had not. But like so many of those other complex diseases that afflict humans, mice don’t naturally get Alzheimer’s. In 1999, Elan Pharmaceuticals created a vaccine to a particular part of amyloid-β and then showed that mice would clear plaques from their brains after treatment with the vaccine. So a few years later, Elan went on to show that anti-amyloid antibodies also cleared plaques in the transgenic mice’s brains when given directly.

After a news conference, the astronauts gave a handful of interviews, and Ars was able to speak with Orion’s pilot, Victor Glover. Glover now stands at the vanguard of our modern Apollo program, named Artemis, which aims to return humans to the Moon and establish a semi-permanent base there. Glover, an accomplished naval aviator, first went to space in November 2020 as the pilot on the first operational Crew Dragon mission to the International Space Station. pages.detailedSummary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched a Cygnus cargo ship from Northrop Grumman on Saturday, kicking off a resupply flight to the International Space Station, Spaceflight Now reports. China has conducted what appears to be a wet dress rehearsal, or fueling test, for its Long March 10B rocket, paving the way for a potential launch within weeks, Space News reports. The US Space Force is still dealing with the near-term implications of the second grounding of United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rocket in less than two years, Ars reports. The Vulcan rocket is one of the two primary launch vehicles the Space Force uses to put satellites into orbit, alongside SpaceX’s Falcon 9. Eric Zarybnisky, head of Space Systems Command’s Space Access office, said the military is “working through a significant number” of potential additional rocket swaps from Vulcan to another launch vehicle, likely SpaceX’s Falcon 9 or Falcon Heavy.

pages.detailedSummary: Text settings Story text Size Small Standard Large Width * Standard Wide Links Standard Orange * Subscribers only Learn more Minimize to nav Oobleck has long been my favorite example of a non-Newtonian fluid, and I’m not alone. In a non-Newtonian fluid, the viscosity changes in response to an applied strain or shearing force, thereby straddling the boundary between liquid and solid behavior. But for non-Newtonian fluids like oobleck, the viscosity changes when a shearing force is applied. By making one bulk measurement, they could track the emergence of the interactions between nanoparticles (the “microscopic frictional contacts”) and correlate that with when the shear thickening behavior kicked in and the liquid switched from liquid to solid behavior. Kim et al., 2023 The team found that the friction between particles was critical to the liquid-to-solid transition, identifying a tipping point when the friction reached a certain level and the viscosity abruptly increased.

pages.detailedSummary: Authors Ivan Belcic Staff writer Cole Stryker Staff Editor, AI Models IBM Think What is GPT (generative pretrained transformer)? Generative pretrained transformers (GPTs) are a family of large language models (LLMs) based on a transformer deep learning architecture. Transformer models including GPT and BERT have powered many notable developments in generative AI since then, with OpenAI’s ChatGPT chatbot taking center stage. The power of GPT models comes from two key aspects: Generative pretraining that teaches the model to detect patterns in unlabeled data, then apply those patterns to new inputs. Transformer models Transformer models are a type of neural network specialized in natural language processing: identifying the intent and meaning in a text-based input.

pages.shortSummary: Proctor In a significant milestone, the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) has completed its 3D map of the Universe—the highest resolution of any such map yet achieved—on schedule and with more data than expected, the collaboration announced today. Analyses of DESI data from earlier runs...
pages.detailedSummary: Proctor In a significant milestone, the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) has completed its 3D map of the Universe—the highest resolution of any such map yet achieved—on schedule and with more data than expected, the collaboration announced today. Analyses of DESI data from earlier runs have already produced exciting hints of new physics—namely that the Universe’s dark energy, rather than being constant, might vary over time. So the Universe should be accelerating much faster than it is if the dark energy is, essentially, the cosmological constant. That data is then sliced into chunks to determine how fast the Universe was expanding at each point of time in the past to better model how dark energy was affecting that expansion. But finishing the original planned survey, on time, with significantly more data than expected, is a major achievement in itself.

pages.detailedSummary: The retraction came from Neurobiology of Aging, which removed a 2011 paper claiming to show that a version of a protein called amyloid-β was responsible for memory loss in Alzheimer’s disease. Alzheimer noted that her brain tissue contained plaques, which had previously been seen in other dementia patients, as well as tangles of nerve fibers, which had not. But like so many of those other complex diseases that afflict humans, mice don’t naturally get Alzheimer’s. In 1999, Elan Pharmaceuticals created a vaccine to a particular part of amyloid-β and then showed that mice would clear plaques from their brains after treatment with the vaccine. So a few years later, Elan went on to show that anti-amyloid antibodies also cleared plaques in the transgenic mice’s brains when given directly.

pages.title: For Empowering Community - DEV Community
pages.shortSummary: And with that introduction, I want to describe the next part of our open source evolution. Forem is open source software for building communities.
pages.detailedSummary: We don’t want to just power community software, we want to empower community. And with that introduction, I want to describe the next part of our open source evolution. Forem is open source software for building communities. As an organization and open source community that works in a distributed, asynchronous style, it's only natural to be somewhat sprawling. Some of the first Forems we launch will be meta-forems designed to help facilitate the software development process as well as the administration, moderation and promotion of individual communities.
